Workbook Management

Complete documentation for workbook operations in Aspose.Cells FOSS for .NET

â†?Back to Index

Workbook Overview

The Workbook class is the root object for all spreadsheet operations in Aspose.Cells FOSS. It represents a complete Excel workbook and provides methods to create, load, modify, and save XLSX files.

Key Features:
  • Create new workbooks from scratch
  • Load existing XLSX files from disk or stream
  • Manage multiple worksheets
  • Access and modify workbook settings
  • Handle document properties and metadata
  • Support for both file and stream operations
  • Resource management through IDisposable pattern

Creating Workbooks

Empty Workbook
// Create a new workbook with one default worksheet
var workbook = new Workbook();

// Access the first worksheet (zero-based index)
var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];

// Save the workbook
workbook.Save("new-workbook.xlsx");
Loading from File
// Load workbook from file path
var workbook = new Workbook("existing-file.xlsx");

// Or specify load options
var options = new LoadOptions();
var workbook = new Workbook("existing-file.xlsx", options);
Loading from Stream
// Load workbook from file stream
using (var stream = new FileStream("data.xlsx", FileMode.Open))
{
    var workbook = new Workbook(stream);

    // Process the workbook
    // ...

    // Save back to stream
    using (var outputStream = new MemoryStream())
    {
        workbook.Save(outputStream, SaveFormat.Xlsx);
    }
}

Saving Workbooks

Basic Save
// Save with default format (XLSX)
workbook.Save("output.xlsx");

// Save with specified format
workbook.Save("output.xlsx", SaveFormat.Xlsx);
Advanced Save Options
// Use SaveOptions for more control
var saveOptions = new SaveOptions
{
    SaveFormat = SaveFormat.Xlsx,
    // Additional options as needed
};

workbook.Save("custom-output.xlsx", saveOptions);
Saving to Stream
// Save to memory stream
using (var stream = new MemoryStream())
{
    workbook.Save(stream, SaveFormat.Xlsx);

    // Use the stream as needed
    byte[] data = stream.ToArray();
}

Workbook Properties

Worksheets Collection
// Access worksheets collection
var worksheets = workbook.Worksheets;

// Get worksheet count
int count = worksheets.Count;

// Access specific worksheet (zero-based index)
var firstSheet = worksheets[0];
var secondSheet = worksheets[1];

// Add new worksheet
var newSheet = worksheets.Add();

// Remove worksheet
worksheets.RemoveAt(2); // Remove worksheet at index 2
Workbook Settings
// Access workbook settings
var settings = workbook.Settings;

// Common settings include:
// - Calculation mode
// - Display settings
// - Protection settings
// - And other workbook-level configurations
Document Properties
// Access document properties
var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;

// Set basic properties
properties.Title = "Annual Report";
properties.Author = "John Doe";
properties.Subject = "Financial Summary";
properties.Comments = "Generated with Aspose.Cells FOSS";

// Get property values
string title = properties.Title;
string author = properties.Author;
Defined Names (Named Ranges)
// Access defined names
var names = workbook.DefinedNames;

// Add a named range
names.Add("SalesData", "Sheet1!$A$1:$D$10");

// Access a named range
var namedRange = names["SalesData"];

Load Options

LoadOptions Overview
// Create load options for control over loading behavior
var options = new LoadOptions
{
    // Configure loading options as needed
    // - Password protection
    // - Data handling
    // - Memory optimization
    // - And other load-time configurations
};

var workbook = new Workbook("protected.xlsx", options);
Load Diagnostics
// Access load diagnostics after loading
var workbook = new Workbook("file-with-issues.xlsx");

if (workbook.LoadDiagnostics != null)
{
    // Check for warnings and errors
    foreach (var warning in workbook.LoadDiagnostics.Warnings)
    {
        Console.WriteLine($"Warning: {warning.Message}");
    }
}

Complete Example

Create and Modify Workbook
using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;
using System;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        // Create a new workbook
        using (var workbook = new Workbook())
        {
            // Access the first worksheet
            var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];

            // Set cell values
            worksheet.Cells["A1"].PutValue("Name");
            worksheet.Cells["B1"].PutValue("Age");
            worksheet.Cells["C1"].PutValue("Salary");

            worksheet.Cells["A2"].PutValue("John Doe");
            worksheet.Cells["B2"].PutValue(30);
            worksheet.Cells["C2"].PutValue(50000.00);

            worksheet.Cells["A3"].PutValue("Jane Smith");
            worksheet.Cells["B3"].PutValue(28);
            worksheet.Cells["C3"].PutValue(55000.00);

            // Set document properties
            workbook.DocumentProperties.Title = "Employee Data";
            workbook.DocumentProperties.Author = "HR Department";
            workbook.DocumentProperties.Comments = "Generated on: " + DateTime.Now;

            // Save the workbook
            workbook.Save("employee-data.xlsx");

            Console.WriteLine("Workbook created successfully!");
        }
    }
}
Load and Process Workbook
using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;
using System;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        // Load existing workbook
        using (var workbook = new Workbook("existing-workbook.xlsx"))
        {
            // Check load diagnostics
            if (workbook.LoadDiagnostics != null &&
                workbook.LoadDiagnostics.Warnings.Count > 0)
            {
                Console.WriteLine("Load warnings:");
                foreach (var warning in workbook.LoadDiagnostics.Warnings)
                {
                    Console.WriteLine($"  - {warning.Message}");
                }
            }

            // Access worksheets
            foreach (var worksheet in workbook.Worksheets)
            {
                Console.WriteLine($"Processing worksheet: {worksheet.Name}");

                // Process each cell in the used range
                for (int row = 0; row <= worksheet.Cells.MaxDataRow; row++)
                {
                    for (int col = 0; col <= worksheet.Cells.MaxDataColumn; col++)
                    {
                        var cell = worksheet.Cells[row, col];
                        if (cell.Value != null)
                        {
                            Console.WriteLine($"  {cell.Name}: {cell.StringValue}");
                        }
                    }
                }
            }

            // Save modified workbook
            workbook.Save("modified-workbook.xlsx");
            Console.WriteLine("Workbook processed successfully!");
        }
    }
}

Important Notes

  • Resource Management: Always use 'using' statements or call Dispose() to properly release workbook resources.
  • File Format: The default save format is XLSX. Specify other formats explicitly using SaveFormat enumeration.
  • Worksheet Indexing: Worksheets are accessed using zero-based indices.
  • Load Diagnostics: Always check LoadDiagnostics after loading files to handle warnings and errors appropriately.
  • Stream Operations: Both loading and saving support streams for better integration with web applications and in-memory processing.
  • Thread Safety: Workbook objects are not thread-safe. Use separate workbook instances for concurrent operations.
